key_insight: A lightweight MobileNetV3 classifier can detect and distinguish pipeline weld defects with very high accuracy, even on noisy RGB images from different capture conditions.
engineering_relevance: The work supports practical on-device weld inspection by combining strong classification performance with low compute, memory, and power needs for mobile or handheld deployment.
method: Preprocess weld images and apply data augmentation to improve robustness to noisy inputs.
Train and compare MobileNetV3 Small and Large for multi-class defect classification.
Evaluate on two real-world datasets collected with HDR cameras and a mobile phone in uncontrolled conditions.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports 99.99% accuracy on the AI5083 dataset and improved performance on PWDID, with about a 3% gain over prior methods.
watch_item: The very small PWDID dataset and the risk of dataset-specific overfitting mean real field deployment should be validated on more diverse inspection conditions.
paper_title: MobileNetV3 for Pipeline Welding Defect Detection: A Computer Vision Approach on Noisy RGB Images
